code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test04-AUG-2017Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting renal involvement, possibly nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy. No evidence of urinary tract infection (negative nitrites, leukocytes). Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for diabetes and renal disease progression.
